sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.9 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finitely generated linear group. a) Is it true that the non-abelian tensor square$G G$is a linear group? b) In particular, is this true for the braid group$B_n$for$n > 3$? It is known that$B_3 B_3$is linear, and there is a countable group$G$such that$G G$is not linear. See the definition of the non-abelian tensor square in (R.\,Brown, J.-L.\,Loday, Topology, { 26}, no.\,3 (1987), 311--3...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
